topics: 🐂 Mishnah: ד׳ וה׳ even when stolen item was slaughtered/sold by other witnesses
🕎 Stolen and sold on Shabbat / for ע״ז / on Yom Kippur — still pay ד׳ וה׳
💀 R' Shimon's exemption: שוחט טריפה and שוחט חולין בעזרה
📜 Principle: כְּדֵי רִשְׁעָתוֹ — court chooses one of two penalties
👥 Zomemim Mishnah: when first or second witness pair is perjured

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Composite Acts and Composite Witnesses
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: The daf has two Mishnayot. The first lists acts that still incur ד׳ וה׳ even when other complicating factors are present — Shabbat, idolatry, Yom Kippur, the father's death, even slaughter for dogs. R' Shimon dissents on two cases. The Halakhah introduces R' Bun bar Chiyya's principle of כְּדֵי רִשְׁעָתוֹ: when two penalties are due, the court selects one. The second Mishnah turns to perjured witnesses — מה משלמין when one or both pairs are zomemim.
overview.hebrewSource: גָּנַב עַל פִּי שְׁנַיִם וְטָבַח וּמָכַר עַל פִּיהֶם אוֹ עַל פִּי שְׁנַיִם אֲחֵרִים מְשַׁלֵּם תַּשְׁלוּמֵי אַרְבָּעָה וַחֲמִשָּׁה

slides.title: 🧾 Mishnah 7:2 — Cases That Still Pay ד׳ וה׳
slides.subtitle: The Mishnah piles up complications and rules each one in.
rows: Stole from father, then father died
גָּנַב מִשֶּׁל אָבִיו... וְאַחַר כָּךְ מֵת אָבִיו

callout.label: The pattern:
callout.text: Even when the slaughter or sale is itself a sin (Shabbat, idolatry, Yom Kippur), the Mishnah still imposes ד׳ וה׳. The act is criminally complete for theft purposes.

slides.title: 💬 Why Shouldn't These Cases Be Exempt?
slides.subtitle: The Yerushalmi raises the principle of כְּדֵי רִשְׁעָתוֹ.

lines.speaker: Q
lines.text: Did not R' Abbahu say in the name of R' Yochanan: הֵזִיד בְּחֵלֶב וְשָׁגַג בְּקָרְבָּן — he is warned, lashed, and brings a sacrifice? So why not double penalty here too?

lines.speaker: RB
lines.text: R' Bun bar Chiyya in the name of R' Shmuel bar R' Yitzchak: כְּדֵי רִשְׁעָתוֹ (Deut. 25:2). Two penalties before the court — pick one.

lines.speaker: Y
lines.text: But heavenly penalties (like a קרבן) are not 'before the court' — so the principle of one-penalty-only does not exclude them.

callout.label: The exegetical move:
callout.text: שְׁנֵי דְבָרִים מְסוּרִין לְבֵית דִּין הַתּוֹפֵס אֶחָד מֵהֶן. When two court-penalties collide, you take one; but a court-penalty + a heavenly-penalty can both stand.

slides.title: 🔪 R' Shimon's Two Exemptions
slides.subtitle: Two slaughters that, for him, do not count as 'slaughter' for ד׳ וה׳.

boxes.title: 🐕 לרפואה / לכלבים
items: R' Shimon: not the normal purpose

boxes.title: 🩸 שחט ונמצא טריפה
items: R' Shimon: a treif slaughter doesn't count

boxes.title: ✅ Tanna kamma
items: The act of שחיטה is the trigger, regardless of purpose

callout.label: What divides them:
callout.text: The Tanna kamma views שחיטה as a discrete act of disposing of the stolen animal. R' Shimon requires that the slaughter actually achieve its halakhic point (kosher meat, normal use).

slides.title: 👥 Mishnah 7:3 — Zomemim Witnesses
slides.subtitle: Who pays whom, and how much, when witnesses are exposed as perjurers.

columns.label: Both pairs zomemim
columns.content:
First pair pays כפל
Second pair pays תשלומי שלושה

columns.label: Only second pair zomemim
columns.content:
He still pays כפל;
they pay תשלומי שלושה

columns.label: One of first pair zomem
columns.content:
בָּטְלָה כָל הָעֵדוּת
everything falls — אם אין גניבה אין טביחה

nodes.content: The structure mirrors the act: theft is the foundation; without it, slaughter and sale collapse.

callout.label: Logical anchor:
callout.text: שֶׁאִם אֵין גְּנֵיבָה אֵין טְבִיחָה וְאֵין מְכִירָה — slaughter/sale presuppose theft. Knock out theft, knock out everything.

slides.title: 🧮 The Math of Triple Restitution
slides.subtitle: Why do the second-pair zomemim pay 'תשלומי שלושה'?

steps.content: 1️⃣ Total liability for stealing + slaughtering an ox = 5×.
2️⃣ Of that, 2× is for the theft alone.
3️⃣ The slaughter/sale witnesses tried to impose the additional 3×.
4️⃣ Therefore when only the second pair is perjured, they pay the 3× they tried to inflict.

callout.label: Bottom line:
callout.text: כאשר עשו — what they tried to do to him is what they pay. Each pair is liable only for the slice of damage their false testimony was attempting to add.

summary.principle: Even compounded acts (Shabbat, ע״ז, Yom Kippur) still pay ד׳ וה׳ — but two court-penalties cannot stack. When witnesses are perjured, each pair pays exactly the slice they tried to impose.

flowSteps.content:
🎓 Key Principle:
Theft is the foundation; if false witnesses are exposed, the structure collapses from the bottom up.

quiz.q: Which of the following does the Mishnah say still incurs ד׳ וה׳?
o: Stealing and slaughtering on Shabbat
Stealing land
Stealing a document
Stealing from a thief
quiz.e: גָּנַב וְטָבַח בְּיוֹם הַכִּיפּוּרִים, on Shabbat, or for ע״ז — all still trigger ד׳ וה׳ per the Mishnah.

quiz.q: What two cases does R' Shimon exempt from ד׳ וה׳?
o: Stealing on Shabbat and stealing on Yom Kippur
Slaughter for medicine/dogs and slaughter that turned out טריפה
Stealing from one's father and dedicating to the Temple
Theft of an ox and theft of a sheep
quiz.e: רִבִּי שִׁמְעוֹן פּוֹטֵר בִּשְׁנֵי אֵילּוּ — לרפואה/לכלבים, and שוחט ונמצא טריפה.

quiz.q: What principle does R' Bun bar Chiyya derive from כְּדֵי רִשְׁעָתוֹ?
o: The court only imposes one of two penalties when both are court-penalties
All sinners are punished twice
Only the wicked are liable for ד׳ וה׳
Penalties are halved on Yom Kippur
quiz.e: שְׁנֵי דְבָרִים מְסוּרִין לְבֵית דִּין הַתּוֹפֵס אֶחָד מֵהֶן. A court-penalty + a heavenly penalty can stack; two court-penalties cannot.

quiz.q: If only the second pair (slaughter/sale witnesses) is shown to be perjured, what do they pay?
o: כפל
תשלומי ארבעה
תשלומי שלושה — the increment from 2× to 5×
Nothing
quiz.e: He still pays כפל for the theft; the second pair pays the תשלומי שלושה they tried falsely to impose.

quiz.q: Why does perjury in the FIRST pair (theft witnesses) collapse the entire case?
o: Because the first pair are senior witnesses
שֶׁאִם אֵין גְּנֵיבָה אֵין טְבִיחָה וְאֵין מְכִירָה — without theft there is nothing to slaughter or sell
Because two pairs cannot testify together
Because of כְּדֵי רִשְׁעָתוֹ
quiz.e: Theft is the foundation. Knock that out and slaughter/sale lose their object — both testimonies are nullified.
